summary: Iteration 2: tested new features (loading skeleton, merged 2h block, queue list toggle, owner-cancel auto-promote, /qr page). Backend: 12/12 pytest cases pass including 2 new tests for auto-promotion (with-queue promotes first, empty-queue deletes). Frontend: skeleton, merged block, /qr page all PASS. Queue-toggle UI verification hit a timing race when re-logging in rapidly between users; underlying functionality works (covered by backend tests + verified by UI rendering of reservation owner change).

integration_issues.issue: After clicking slot-join-queue-{hour}, the join API may not complete before the next localStorage.clear()+reload sequence. Same UX timing race already noted in iteration_1. Recommend awaiting the join-queue mutation success state (e.g., disable button + spinner) before allowing user switch; or short delay (500ms) after click before navigating away.

action_items: Optional UX: show a brief loading state on Join Queue button + await mutation before any navigation, to harden the multi-user race
critical_code_review_comments: cancel_reservation auto-promote logic is correct: when queue non-empty, updates user_name=queue[0] and queue=queue[1:], returns {promoted:true,new_owner}; when empty, deletes and returns {promoted:false}.
Schedule.jsx skip-continuation loop (h += res.duration) properly merges visually so only one slot-row at start_hour exists for a 2h booking; verified slot-row-10 is absent.
SlotRow merged block shows time range (09:00-11:00) and duration label (2h); queue toggle conditionally renders only when queueCount>0; data-testids are correctly suffixed by hour.
QRPage uses QRCodeSVG with window.location.origin and has back link + print button with proper testids and print:hidden header — clean.
Loading skeleton (data-testid slot-skeleton) renders when reservations===null and is replaced by slot-list after fetch — confirmed via throttled network test.
Minor: client-side cancel handler uses res?.promoted from API response — good. Backend returns shape {success, promoted, new_owner?}.

success_rate.frontend: 5/6 verified (skeleton, 2h merged block, time/duration label, /qr page+back, owner-cancel-promote backend; queue-list UI not asserted due to test timing — but underlying API + rendering paths verified separately)

test_credentials: Name-based login (no password); used Alex/Bob/Casey + TEST_* names
seed_data_creation: Tests create/cleanup reservations on date today+30 with TEST_ prefix; UI test cleans up today's reservations via API before/after.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_reservations.py now include test_cancel_with_queue_promotes_first_in_queue and test_cancel_with_empty_queue_deletes. New data-testids in this iteration: slot-skeleton, slot-list, slot-queue-toggle-{hour}, slot-queue-list-{hour}, qr-link, qr-page, qr-back-link, qr-print-button, qr-title, qr-image, qr-url. /qr route is public (no auth gate).
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking bugs. Frontend test 4 timing flake is a test harness issue, not a product bug; product UX could optionally be hardened by awaiting mutation before allowing user switch.
